title: How People Stop Thinking When Using AI
summary: Scientists studied how people use AI like chatbots. They found that many people trust AI too much and stop thinking for themselves, which is called "cognitive surrender." In experiments, people were given tricky questions. Some could ask an AI for help, but the AI sometimes gave wrong answers on purpose. Even when the AI was wrong, many people still believed it. This shows that AI can make people lazy thinkers. When people were paid or had more time, they checked the AI's answers better. But when they were rushed, they trusted the AI more. This research warns us to be careful with AI and always think critically.

tests scientists do to learn things

using your brain to check if something makes sense

Did the AI always give correct answers in the tests?
What happened when people were rushed for time?
What helped people check AI answers better?
options: Getting paid or having more time
questions.correct_answer: Getting paid or having more time

questions.question: Why is it important to think critically with AI?
options: To avoid mistakes from wrong answers
questions.correct_answer: To avoid mistakes from wrong answers

background_read: AI, or artificial intelligence, is technology that can answer questions or do tasks like humans. Scientists study how people use AI to understand if it helps or hurts thinking. In this research, they did tests with people and AI to see if people trust AI too much, even when it's wrong. This is important because using AI without thinking can lead to mistakes in school or life. Learning to check AI answers helps us stay smart and safe.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from the University of Pennsylvania
WHAT: Studied how people trust AI too much and stop thinking, called cognitive surrender
WHY: To warn about the dangers of not thinking critically with AI

perspectives.description: Some people see AI as a useful tool that can assist with tasks, but it needs checking.
Others worry that relying too much on AI can make people lazy and cause errors.
